LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, JUN-16-2020 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT EDWARD HIGGINBOTHAM'S MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT HIGGINBOTHAM'S MOTION TO VACATE THE DEFAULT ENTERED ON FEBRUARY 20, 2020 IS GRANTED ? THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE IS AN APPEARANCE/PLEADING THAT PREVENTS ENTRY OF THE DEFAULT. (SEE CODE CIV. PROC. 1014, 585.) DEFENDANT MAY RE-SET THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE FOR HEARING AFTER THE DEFAULT IS VACATED. THE COURT INSTRUCTS DEFENSE COUNSEL TO EMAIL A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Recover Fees And Costs Under The Anti-Slapp Statute
Off Calendar Jan-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. GLEN BROEMER, Esq. (Tel: 805-663-2654) for Plaintiff Sally Liu;
Atty. CONRAD K. WU, Esq. (Tel: 415-581-0885) for Defendant Edward M. Higginbotham;
Atty. EDWARD M. HIGGINBOTHAM, Esq. (Tel: 415-581-0885) for Defendants Jose Ceron, Espetacion Monique Medina, Brian Rangel, and Judy Judkins.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS JOSE CERON, ESPETACION MEDINA, AND JUDY JUDKINS' Motion To Recover Fees And Costs Under The Anti-Slapp Statute.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants Jose Ceron, Espetacion Monique Medina and Judy Judkins' motion "to recover fees and costs under the anti-SLAPP statute" is granted in the amount of $32,000. I reviewed the attorney billing records presented; they are reasonable. An enhancement for the contingency nature of the representation is included in the award. (Ketchum v. Moses (2001) 24 Cal.4th 1122, 1132.)
Order signed. Defendants may submit a separate proposed judgment to the court for review and signature.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Christine L. Jordan, CSR No. 12262, cjordan@sftc.org (302/RBU)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jun-16-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. Glen R. Broemer, Esq. of LAW OFFICES OF GLEN R. BROEMER (Tel: 805-663-2654) appearing via CourtCall for plaintiff Sally Liu;
Atty. Conrad K. Wu, Esq. of LAW OFFICES OF PAUL L. ALAGA (Tel: 415-581-0885) appearing via CourtCall for Defendant Edward M. Higginbotham.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT EDWARD HIGGINBOTHAM'S MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel for parties.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Higginbotham's motion to vacate the default entered on February 20, 2020 is granted. "It is the policy of the law to favor, whenever possible, a hearing on the merits . . . Therefore, when a party in default moves promptly to seek relief, very slight evidence is required to justify a trial court's order setting aside a default." (Shamblin v. Brattain (1988) 44 Cal.3d 474, 478.) Defendant filed this motion within a reasonable time. The court entered the default on February 20, 2020 and defendant filed this motion on March 11, 2020. Defendant establishes that the default was entered as the result of mistake, inadvertence, excusable neglect, and/or surprise. Defendant submitted his anti-SLAPP motion to strike for filing on February 18, 2020 (prior to entry of the default) and the court rejected the filing because it failed to include a notice of posting court reporter fees. (Wu Dec., par. 4.) Defendant attempted to cure the mistake, but it ended up being too late. The oversight regarding the court reporter fee notice was excusable given the liberal policy to hear cases on the merits. Lastly, defendant attaches a proposed pleading-the anti-SLAPP motion to strike-and therefore relief is warranted. (Wu Dec., Ex. D.)

None of plaintiff's opposing arguments compel denial of the motion. The motion sufficiently identifies Code of Civil Procedure 473 as the basis for the motion and defendant is entitled to discretionary relief. The anti-SLAPP motion to strike is an appearance/pleading that prevents entry of the default. (See Code Civ. Proc. 1014, 585.) Defendant may re-set the anti-SLAPP motion to strike for hearing after the default is vacated.

The Court instructs defense counsel to email a proposed order to the Court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
